coreTruthSet.description: Material culture is framed as preserved behavior carrying evidence of skill, belief, and social necessity.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Material culture isn’t just “objects.” It’s frozen behavior...

coreTruthSet.description: Caddoan pottery in the Natchitoches world is represented as highly refined, thin-walled, and design-rich.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Caddoan pottery—used by the Natchitoches—was among the most refined in North America.

coreTruthSet.description: Tool systems are presented as local, efficient, and category-diverse across food, hunting, craft, and woodworking.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The Natchitoches tool system was efficient, adaptable, and local.

coreTruthSet.description: Pottery production is described as multigenerational technical knowledge, likely transmitted heavily through women’s labor systems.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: This knowledge was passed down through generations... likely held primarily by women.

settingAnchors.name: Natchitoches Material-Culture Zone
settingAnchors.description: Research scope for production, use, and meaning of pottery, tools, and housing materials in the Natchitoches world.

continuityConstraints.title: Function and Identity Object Logic
continuityConstraints.text: Objects carry functional and identity load at once; utility, beauty, and meaning are not separable in cultural analysis.

continuityConstraints.title: Local Material Housing Adaptation
continuityConstraints.text: Housing form follows local ecological materials and maintenance logic, emphasizing adaptation and repairability.

continuityConstraints.title: Pottery as Knowledge Transmission
continuityConstraints.text: Pottery craft reflects stored technical memory (materials, firing, design) transmitted through generational practice.

openResearchGaps.title: What seasonal or status variation existed in housing materials and construction patterns?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to avoid over-uniform dwelling descriptions in chapter environments.

openResearchGaps.title: Which specific pottery design motifs can be tied to identifiable Natchitoches or related Caddoan communities?
openResearchGaps.description: Need source-anchored motif taxonomy before assigning symbolic meanings to named lineages or locations.

openResearchGaps.title: How much of the described tool kit reflects local production versus trade diffusion?
openResearchGaps.description: Differentiate locally fabricated technologies from imported/adapted tools for scene-level material realism.
